title: Possibly bad news for Shin
wikitext:
[]: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by FluffyBunny » Mon Feb 01, 2016 7:24 pm This bit of news is going around on Korean sites. They say Tatsuo Sato tweeted the following ( https://twitter.com/seitenhyohyo/status ... 7248177152 ): そもそも現場をバラしてて劇場も何も無いじゃない。ゆっくり待てばいいと思うよ

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 01, 2016 7:27 pm Wasn't there something recently about them moving office? This could be no more than that, perhaps? Yes, read here . So it's just piling speculation on speculation...

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by FluffyBunny » Mon Feb 01, 2016 7:42 pm Ah, thank you very much for that. "The work site being torn down" makes a lot more sense that way -- compared to the speculation I brought here.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Feb 02, 2016 12:31 am Just sounds like any production work on Final - of which there undoubtably has been some even if it's merely design work - is on hold while Khara changes their location.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Feb 02, 2016 12:12 pm I'm not so worried about this. We've been waiting long enough, another year makes no difference.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Feb 02, 2016 1:10 pm Would make sense with the recent recruitment drive they've had on their site and twitter. New facility, new/extra staff.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Feb 04, 2016 4:01 pm Unaware if it's been said before (probably) but I'm sure Anno's work on Godzilla Resurgence is slowing down Final too.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by robersora » Thu Feb 04, 2016 4:21 pm xtr00kvltcorex wrote: Unaware if it's been said before (probably) but I'm sure Anno's work on Godzilla Resurgence is slowing down Final too.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Feb 04, 2016 4:41 pm Hopefully we get Final at least by the 20 year anniversary of EoE. Basically Khara just needs (I wish) to get on with it already.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Feb 04, 2016 4:54 pm I wouldn't be surprised if it's out summer 2017. That's the 20th anniversary of End and the 10th anniversary of 1.0.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Feb 04, 2016 5:36 pm Didn't they plan on having it all done by 2012 or something close to that originally? (lol)

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by hui43210 » Thu Feb 04, 2016 5:40 pm Remember, as far as we've been told work has been done on Shin at the same time as Godzillia. Postby The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 25, 2015 4:22 pm I spoke with a source very close to production at Khara. Though Anno is working on Godzilla, production on the final Rebuild film is active and ongoing. The wheels are in fact turning.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by Sicarius VI » Thu Feb 04, 2016 5:43 pm ^^The original plan for rebuild was for 1.0 out in 2007 then 2.0 out a year later in 2008. Which it was delayed till 2009. The kicker to all of this is that 3.0 and 4.0(which it was called at the time) where going to be a double release being both 45 minutes long and being released in 2012 IIRC. We only of course got 3.0 in 2012, and now it looks like we are going towards a 4 year gap in between 3 and 3+1. This is of course if 3+1 comes out in 2017 because by November of this year 3.0 will be 4 years old. This isn't to say some assume it will be out mid/late 2016 but then they also said that about 2015... (Winter of 2015 was a date for a while, so was Winter of 2014). Edit- Ninja'd. ^ Lets hope we start seeing some of that "production" later this year.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Feb 04, 2016 5:54 pm Fingers crossed for next year at the very least. Almost as bad as waiting for another A Song of Ice and Fire book. Almost.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Feb 04, 2016 6:06 pm Well, there's always a chance something will pop up at this year's paper lantern festival. It's always neat to see something from Anno himself whether it's Eva-related or not. I think the wait seems a lot worse because Khara is so quiet about their productions compared to US studios. Over here you get bombarded with status updates and the like for nearly two years before a movie comes out. Khara can go silent for ages and them boom, trailer. Or a mural on the front of a store.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Feb 05, 2016 2:12 am The big difference with Khara's production is they aren't setting specific release dates until they're good and ready. Hollywood productions are all about marking out that weekend for their movie. Case in point, no one outside the inner circle knows what movie Pixar has planned for 2020 but they've already marked March 13th as the film's release date. Khara just plays it by ear. I kinda love that. As for Final, I won't make any assumptions on when it's coming out. For now, I'll take refuge in the fact that Anno's next movie WILL happen - in Japan at least - on July 29th this year.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 05, 2016 4:44 am I'm currently expecting the last film in late autumn 2017, five years on from Q . I hope that's realistic enough that I'm as likely to be pleasantly surprised by the eventual reality as otherwise.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Fri Feb 05, 2016 12:39 pm Didn't Q just kinda drop out of nowhere? Correct me if I'm wrong. Gives me hope for the future if true

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Feb 05, 2016 1:10 pm ^ Not really. On New Years Day 2012, Khara posted a graphic of Asuka in her space suit - complete with cat ears - and released the film's title was 'You Can (Not) Redo.' I can't remember if it was there too but I think they're graphic also said something along the lines of "Coming out in 2012". Then over the summer, in July I think, they had an event where they screened a preview that gave out the release date of November 17th. It's not the Hollywood "Announce a release date three years out" but there is a little warm-up before the film gets released. Once they announce what the subtitle of Final is - I'm really hoping for 'You Can (Not) Run Away." - we'll know the release of the film will be within the next 12 months.

[]: Re: Possibly bad news for Shin? Post by robersora » Fri Feb 05, 2016 1:45 pm ^ The Cat helmeted Asuka even appeared before that in April, if I recall correctly. The advertising of the rebuilds was never bad, just very reserved compared to Hollywood movies.
